mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the policy stance is restrictive enough to compress corporate valuations via higher discount rates and tighter credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the unemployment rate reflects deteriorating labor market conditions that foreshadow slowing consumption and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE trend and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns following rising UNRATE periods are not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in payroll employment growth and elevated CPI inflation signals an overheating economy that raises the probability of continued monetary tightening, jointly producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=301.821 revised, PAYEMS=155134 thousand as of 2023-03-01
failure_condition: Joint interaction term (PAYEMS growth x CPIAUCSL YoY) shows no incremental predictive power over either series individually (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10) across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in retail sales reflects robust consumer demand that supports near-term corporate revenue and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS month-over-month growth are not significantly higher than following negative growth (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output consistent with an emerging supply-side slowdown or recession, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Conditional on INDPRO YoY < 0%, 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative relative to unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or triggered instances yield positive returns > 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime with FEDFUNDS above 4%, a decline in housing starts amplifies recession risk signaling because rate-sensitive construction activity falls faster, jointly producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days that exceed the effect of either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from 1450 to 1399 thousand units (revised) around 2023-02-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: Interaction term (HOUST decline x FEDFUNDS level) does not add significant predictive power over the additive model (p > 0.10) in walk-forward validation, or triggered periods show non-negative average 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-style trigger, where the 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises 0.3 percentage points or more above its trailing 12-month low, signals the onset of recession and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: 3-month MA of UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above trailing 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.5% on 2023-03-01, historically low and near cycle trough
failure_condition: Triggered Sahm-rule events are not followed by significantly negative 60-day returns (p > 0.05), or the signal fires with false positive rate exceeding 50% across historical walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate stabilizes at a cycle peak after a prior period of rapid hikes, markets begin pricing in an eventual easing cycle, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premia compress in anticipation of rate cuts.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS unchanged for 2 consecutive months after prior 12-month increase > 3pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=4.65% on 2023-03-01 following rapid 2022-2023 hiking cycle
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during rate-pause periods are not significantly positive versus baseline (p > 0.05), or such pauses are followed by renewed hikes and negative returns in more than 50% of historical instances.
